title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
During the tea service at the Doveton villa, Mr. Satterthwaite experiences a sudden sense of paranoia and intuition regarding an impending crisis. He observes Beryl Gillett accidentally break a red teacup and replace it with a light blue one from a tray, while also repositioning an opalite pipe near the new cups. When Timothy approaches the table, Inez mistakenly identifies the blue cup as hers and the red one as his, but Timothy insists the cup containing sugar is his.

Sensing immediate danger, Mr. Satterthwaite rushes forward and shouts for Timothy not to drink. He explains to a bewildered Dr. Hodton that Timothy is color blind and has been deceived by the cup swap. During the confrontation, Mr. Satterthwaite reveals a realization regarding parentage, claiming that Timothy is the son of Lily, while Roland is the son of Bertrill and Christopher Eden. He alleges that a woman intentionally purchased these specific teacups to facilitate a poisoning, knowing Timothy could not distinguish the colors.

Dr. Hodton takes the blue cup from Timothy under the guise of examining a flaw in the porcelain. As the men walk toward the gate, Beryl Gillett rushes toward the house in an anxious and fearful state. She encounters Tom Addison and sharply points out that he is wearing mismatched shoes—one red and one green—noting that he cannot see the difference. Beryl then flees the scene. As Mr. Satterthwaite and Dr. Hodton watch a motorcycle speed away, Mr. Satterthwaite concludes that Beryl's departure is likely for the best.
